settings.title: Five Hundred Sessions and Counting
settings.excerpt: This week we coached our five hundredth client session since opening the studio, and one client has been here for all of it.
settings.body:
This week we quietly passed five hundred coached sessions at the Ironform studio. It is the kind of milestone that creeps up on you when you are busy counting reps instead of weeks.

What we are proudest of is not the number itself but who is in it. One client started in our very first month, recovering from a knee operation, and now deadlifts more than she ever thought possible. She still trains on the same morning slot she booked all those weeks ago.

What five hundred sessions actually looks like

Five hundred sessions is not five hundred dramatic days. It is mostly ordinary ones. A warm up, a couple of working sets, a small note in a logbook, and a plan for next time. The progress hides inside that repetition, and you only really see it when you look back.

When we read through the old training logs to mark the occasion, a few patterns stood out across almost every client who stuck around:

  • They came back even on the weeks they did not feel like it.
  • They added weight slowly rather than chasing big jumps.
  • They treated a missed session as a gap, not a failure.

How we are saying thank you

To celebrate, we are donating a session fee for every new sign up this month to the local youth sports club, which gives kids access to coaching many families could not otherwise afford. It felt right to mark our own count by helping someone else get started.

If you have been thinking about starting, this is a gentle time to do it. You do not need to be fit first, and you will not be the only beginner on the floor.

Here is to the next five hundred, and to every person who keeps showing up.
